summary: Iteration 71: Incident Tickets System - All 27 tests PASSED. Verified the complete incident ticket lifecycle: (1) accept-receipt with action='accept_with_incident' creates incident ticket, (2) accept-receipt with action='accept' logs audit but no ticket, (3) GET /api/incident-tickets and /summary work correctly, (4) Ticket detail includes timeline with created event, (5) assign, add-note, resolve, close all work correctly with proper status transitions and timeline events.

tests: test_09: action='accept_with_incident' creates incident ticket and returns ticket_id/ticket_number
test_23: action='accept' does NOT create incident ticket
FEATURE_1_ACCEPT_RECEIPT_WITH_ACTION.implementation: branch_transfers.py line 851-1041: accept_receipt checks action field, creates incident_tickets doc when 'accept_with_incident'

tests: test_01-04: List and summary endpoints work
test_10: GET /{id} returns ticket detail with timeline
test_13-15: PUT /add-note adds note to timeline, validates empty note
test_16-17: PUT /resolve sets status to 'resolved' with note and recovery_amount
test_18-19: PUT /close sets status to 'closed' (admin only)
FEATURE_2_INCIDENT_TICKETS_CRUD.implementation: routes/incident_tickets.py - full module with all CRUD endpoints

FEATURE_3_TIMELINE_TRACKING.description: Each incident ticket maintains a timeline of events
tests: test_10: Timeline has 'created' event on ticket creation
test_12: Timeline has 'assigned' event after assign
test_14: Timeline has 'note' events after add-note
test_19: Timeline has 'closed' event after close
FEATURE_3_TIMELINE_TRACKING.implementation: Each API pushes event objects with {action, by_id, by_name, detail, at} to timeline array

FEATURE_4_AUDIT_LOGGING.description: Variance acceptance and incident resolution logged to audit_log collection

critical_code_review_comments: All incident ticket APIs correctly implemented with proper status transitions: open → investigating → resolved → closed
Timeline events are properly tracked with action, by_id, by_name, detail, and timestamp
Accept-receipt correctly handles both 'accept' and 'accept_with_incident' actions
Role-based access control: assign/resolve require manager or admin, close requires admin only
Audit logging happens for variance acceptance and incident resolution
seed_data_creation: Created TEST incident ticket via transfer BTO-20260227-0053 with 5-unit shortage. Added inventory to Main Branch for test product.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 71 verified the incident tickets system: (1) incident_tickets collection stores tickets created via accept-receipt with action='accept_with_incident', (2) Full lifecycle tested: create → assign → add-note → resolve → close, (3) Summary endpoint returns status counts and unresolved losses, (4) Audit logs written for variance_accepted and incident_resolved events. Database now has 1 closed incident ticket INC-00001. Test product ID: 58ae683d-6067-406e-bf47-a091bb2152ab has inventory in Main Branch.
